Received: "# claude.md

this file provides guidance to claude code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## purpose

this is an **end-to-end test fixture repository** for the occ test harness. it is not a real application — it exists to provide a deterministic, replayable environment for e2e tests of agent behavior (e.g. the `/init` flow). the repo pairs a tiny "demo" project with recorded llm streaming responses so tests can replay multi-turn agent conversations without hitting a live model.

## structure

- `package.json` + `src/math.js` — a minimal demo project. the `test` script (`npm test`) just runs `node src/math.js`, which parses and exits; it exists so tests have something to "build/run", not to assert anything.
- `fixtures/*.json` — recorded agent responses, one per conversation turn. each fixture has:
  - `input`: array of conversation turns. the first element is the user's prompt (e.g. the `/init` command). subsequent elements come in `[tool_use...]` / `[tool_result...]` pairs that capture the tool round-trip between turns.
  - `output`: array of streamed response events (`message_start`, `content_block_start/delta/stop`, `assistant` snapshots, `message_delta`, `message_stop`) — the exact sse-style stream the model produced, including `thinking` and `tool_use` blocks.

### fixture naming and chaining

fixtures are named `<id>.json` for the first turn and `<id>-<suffix>.json` for subsequent turns of the same conversation. they share the same `<id>` so a test can walk the chain: turn 1 reads `131c3c.json`, turn 2 (after tool results) reads `131c3c-08f458-4619f4.json`, etc.

### the `[cwd]` placeholder

tool inputs in fixtures use the literal token `[cwd]` (e.g. `"command": "ls -la [cwd]/"`) instead of the real working-directory path. the test harness substitutes `[cwd]` with the actual temp path at replay time, which is why fixtures are path-portable despite being recorded against `/tmp/occ-e2e-oufnen`.

## working in this repo

- **do not "fix" the demo code or add features to `src/math.js`.** its triviality is the point — it's stage dressing for the e2e scenario, not production code.
- **do not regenerate fixtures against a live model** unless that is the explicit task. fixtures are checked-in expected output; editing them changes what the e2e suite asserts.
- when modifying a fixture, preserve the streaming event structure exactly: the event `type`s, the `index` ordering of content blocks, and the `assistant` snapshot entries (which carry `uuid`/`requestid`/`timestamp`) are all part of what the replay harness validates.

this file provides guidance to claude code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## purpose

this is an **end-to-end test fixture repository** for the occ test harness. it is not a real application — it exists to provide a deterministic, replayable environment for e2e tests of agent behavior (e.g. the `/init` flow). the repo pairs a tiny "demo" project with recorded llm streaming responses so tests can replay multi-turn agent conversations without hitting a live model.

## structure

- `package.json` + `src/math.js` — a minimal demo project. the `test` script (`npm test`) just runs `node src/math.js`, which parses and exits; it exists so tests have something to "build/run", not to assert anything.
- `fixtures/*.json` — recorded agent responses, one per conversation turn. each fixture has:
  - `input`: array of conversation turns. the first element is the user's prompt (e.g. the `/init` command). subsequent elements come in `[tool_use...]` / `[tool_result...]` pairs that capture the tool round-trip between turns.
  - `output`: array of streamed response events (`message_start`, `content_block_start/delta/stop`, `assistant` snapshots, `message_delta`, `message_stop`) — the exact sse-style stream the model produced, including `thinking` and `tool_use` blocks.

### fixture naming and chaining

fixtures are named `<id>.json` for the first turn and `<id>-<suffix>.json` for subsequent turns of the same conversation. they share the same `<id>` so a test can walk the chain: turn 1 reads `131c3c.json`, turn 2 (after tool results) reads `131c3c-08f458-4619f4.json`, etc.

### the `[cwd]` placeholder

tool inputs in fixtures use the literal token `[cwd]` (e.g. `"command": "ls -la [cwd]/"`) instead of the real working-directory path. the test harness substitutes `[cwd]` with the actual temp path at replay time, which is why fixtures are path-portable despite being recorded against `/tmp/occ-e2e-oufnen`.

## working in this repo

- **do not "fix" the demo code or add features to `src/math.js`.** its triviality is the point — it's stage dressing for the e2e scenario, not production code.
- **do not regenerate fixtures against a live model** unless that is the explicit task. fixtures are checked-in expected output; editing them changes what the e2e suite asserts.
- when modifying a fixture, preserve the streaming event structure exactly: the event `type`s, the `index` ordering of content blocks, and the `assistant` snapshot entries (which carry `uuid`/`requestid`/`timestamp`) are all part of what the replay harness validates.
